heading: Solar energy research and development

children.heading: Critical material
children.label: 1 Critical material
children.text: The term “critical material” has the meaning given the term in .section 1606 of title 30

children.heading: Economically distressed area
children.label: 2 Economically distressed area
children.text: The term “economically distressed area” means an area described in .section 3161(a) of this title

children.label: 4 Indian Tribe
children.text: The term “Indian Tribe” has the meaning given the term in .section 5304 of title 25

children.heading: Minority-serving institution
children.label: 7 Minority-serving institution
children.text: The term “minority-serving institution” has the meaning given the term “eligible institution” in .section 1067q(a) of title 20

children.label: 8 National Laboratory
children.text: The term “National Laboratory” has the meaning given such term in .section 15801(3) of this title

children.label: 9 Native Hawaiian community-based organization
children.text: The term “Native Hawaiian community-based organization” has the meaning given the term in .section 7517 of title 20

children.heading: Program
children.label: 11 Program
children.text: The term “program” means the program established under subsection (b)(1)(A).

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 12 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Energy.

children.label: A
children.text: thermal or electric energy derived from radiation from the Sun; or

children.label: B
children.text: energy resulting from a chemical reaction caused by radiation recently originated in the Sun.

children.heading: Territory or freely associated state
children.label: 14 Territory or freely associated state
children.text: The term “territory or freely associated state” has the meaning given the term “insular area” in .section 3103 of title 7

children.heading: Tribal energy development organization
children.label: 15 Tribal energy development organization
children.text: The term “Tribal energy development organization” has the meaning given the term “tribal energy development organization” in .section 3501 of title 25

children.heading: Tribal organization
children.label: 16 Tribal organization
children.text: The term “Tribal organization” has the meaning given the term in .section 5304 of title 25

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a program to conduct research, development, demonstration, and commercialization of solar energy technologies in accordance with this subsection.

children.label: i
children.text: To improve the energy efficiency, cost effectiveness, reliability, resilience, security, siting, integration, manufacturability, installation, decommissioning, and recyclability of solar energy technologies.

children.label: ii
children.text: To optimize the performance and operation of solar energy components, cells, and systems, and enabling technologies, including through the development of new materials, hardware, and software.

children.label: iii
children.text: To optimize the design and adaptability of solar energy systems to the broadest practical range of geographic and atmospheric conditions.

children.label: iv
children.text: To support the integration of solar energy technologies with the electric grid and complementary energy technologies.

children.label: v
children.text: To create and improve the conversion of solar energy to other useful forms of energy or other products.

children.label: vi
children.text: To reduce the cost, risk, and other potential negative impacts across the lifespan of solar energy technologies, including manufacturing, siting, permitting, installation, operations, maintenance, decommissioning, and recycling.

children.label: vii
children.text: To reduce and mitigate potential life cycle negative impacts of solar energy technologies on human communities, wildlife, and wildlife habitats.

children.label: viii
children.text: To address barriers to the commercialization and export of solar energy technologies.

children.label: ix
children.text: To support the domestic solar industry, workforce, and supply chain.

children.heading: Targets
children.label: C Targets
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the Secretary shall establish targets for the program to address near-term (up to 2 years), mid-term (up to 7 years), and long-term (up to 15 years) challenges to the advancement of all types of solar energy systems.December 27, 2020

children.label: i
children.text: awarding grants and awards, on a competitive, merit-reviewed basis;

children.label: ii
children.text: performing precompetitive research and development;

children.label: iii
children.text: establishing or maintaining demonstration facilities and projects, including through stewardship of existing facilities;

children.label: iv
children.text: providing technical assistance;

children.label: v
children.text: entering into contracts and cooperative agreements;

children.label: vi
children.text: providing small business vouchers;

children.label: vii
children.text: establishing prize competitions;

children.label: viii
children.text: conducting education and outreach activities;

children.label: ix
children.text: conducting workforce development activities; and

children.label: x
children.text: conducting analyses, studies, and reports.

children.label: I
children.text: new materials, components, designs, and systems, including perovskites, cadmium telluride, and organic materials;

children.label: II
children.text: advanced photovoltaic and thin-film devices;

children.label: III
children.text: concentrated solar power;

children.label: IV
children.text: solar heating and cooling; and

children.label: V
children.text: enabling technologies for solar energy systems, including hardware and software.

children.label: ii
children.text: Solar energy technology siting, performance, installation, operations, resilience, and security.

children.label: I
children.text: the electric grid, including transmission, distribution, microgrids, and distributed energy systems;

children.label: aa
children.text: other generation sources;

children.label: bb
children.text: demand response technologies; and

children.label: cc
children.text: energy storage technologies; and

children.label: III
children.text: other applications, such as in the agriculture, transportation, buildings, industrial, and fuels sectors.

children.label: iv
children.text: Advanced solar energy manufacturing technologies and practices, including materials, processes, and design.

children.label: v
children.text: Methods to improve the lifetime, maintenance, decommissioning, recycling, reuse, and sustainability of solar energy components and systems, including technologies and strategies that reduce the use of energy, water, critical materials, and other commodities that are determined to be vulnerable to disruption.

children.label: vi
children.text: Solar energy forecasting, modeling, and atmospheric measurement systems, including for small-scale, large-scale, and aggregated systems.

children.label: I
children.text: generation sources;

children.label: II
children.text: loads; and

children.label: III
children.text: storage technologies.

children.label: I
children.text: distributed and community solar technologies, including the development of best practices, models, and voluntary streamlined processes for local siting and permitting of distributed solar energy systems to reduce costs;

children.label: II
children.text: local communities, with special consideration given to economically distressed areas, previously disturbed lands such as landfills and former mines, and other areas disproportionately impacted by environmental pollution;

children.label: III
children.text: wildlife and wildlife habitats; and

children.label: IV
children.text: any other appropriate matter, as determined by the Secretary.

children.label: ix
children.text: Transformational technologies for harnessing solar energy.

children.label: x
children.text: Other research areas that advance the purposes of the program, as determined by the Secretary.

children.label: i
children.text: are located in a geographically diverse range of eligible entities;

children.label: I
children.text: in economically distressed areas and areas disproportionately impacted by pollution; or

children.label: II
children.text: that provide the greatest potential to reduce energy costs, as well as promote accessibility and community implementation of demonstrated technologies;

children.label: iii
children.text: can be replicated in a variety of regions and climates;

children.label: I
children.text: domestic manufacturing and production of solar energy technologies; or

children.label: II
children.text: exports of solar energy technologies;

children.label: v
children.text: are carried out in collaboration with Tribal energy development organizations, Indian Tribes, Tribal organizations, Native Hawaiian community-based organizations, minority-serving institutions, or territories or freely associated States; and

children.label: vi
children.text: with regards to workforce development, activities that expand the number of individuals from underrepresented groups pursuing and attaining skills relevant to solar energy.

children.heading: Coordination
children.label: D Coordination
children.text: To the maximum extent practicable, the Secretary shall coordinate activities under the program with other relevant programs and capabilities of the Department and other Federal research programs.

children.heading: Use of funds
children.label: E Use of funds
children.text: To the extent that funding is not otherwise available through other Federal programs or power purchase agreements, funding awarded for demonstration projects may be used for additional nontechnology costs, as determined to be appropriate by the Secretary, such as engineering or feasibility studies.

children.heading: Solicitation
children.label: F Solicitation
children.text: Not less than once every two years, the Secretary shall conduct a national solicitation for applications for demonstration projects under this section.

children.heading: Grants
children.label: A Grants
children.text: In addition to the program activities described in paragraph (2), in carrying out the program, the Secretary shall award financial assistance to eligible entities for research, development, demonstration, and commercialization projects to advance new solar energy manufacturing technologies and techniques.

children.label: I
children.text: the manufacturing process; and

children.label: II
children.text: the use of resources, such as energy, water, and critical materials;

children.label: ii
children.text: support domestic supply chains for materials and components;

children.label: iii
children.text: identify and incorporate nonhazardous alternative materials for components and devices;

children.label: iv
children.text: operate in partnership with Tribal energy development organizations, Indian Tribes, Tribal organizations, Native Hawaiian community-based organizations, minority-serving institutions, or territories or freely associated states; or

children.label: v
children.text: are located in economically distressed areas.

children.heading: Evaluation
children.label: C Evaluation
children.text: Not later than 3 years after , and every 4 years thereafter, the Secretary shall conduct, and make available to the public and the relevant committees of Congress, an independent review of the progress of the grants awarded under subparagraph (A).December 27, 2020

children.heading: Solar energy technology recycling research, development, and demonstration program
children.label: 4 Solar energy technology recycling research, development, and demonstration program
i
children.text: by increasing the efficiency and cost effectiveness of the recovery of raw materials from solar energy technology components and systems, including enabling technologies such as inverters;

children.label: ii
children.text: by minimizing potential environmental impacts from the recovery and disposal processes;

children.label: iii
children.text: by advancing technologies and processes for the disassembly and recycling of solar energy devices;

children.label: iv
children.text: by developing alternative materials, designs, manufacturing processes, and other aspects of solar energy technologies and the disassembly and resource recovery process that enable efficient, cost effective, and environmentally responsible disassembly of, and resource recovery from, solar energy technologies; and

children.label: v
children.text: strategies to increase consumer acceptance of, and participation in, the recycling of photovoltaic devices.

children.label: i
children.text: development of best practices or training materials for use in the photovoltaics manufacturing, design, installation, refurbishing, disposal, or recycling industries;

children.label: ii
children.text: dissemination at industry conferences;

children.label: iii
children.text: coordination with information dissemination programs relating to recycling of electronic devices in general;

children.label: iv
children.text: demonstration projects; and

children.label: v
children.text: educational materials.

children.heading: Priority
children.label: C Priority
children.text: In carrying out the activities authorized under this subsection, the Secretary shall give special consideration to projects that recover critical materials.

children.heading: Sensitive information
children.label: D Sensitive information
children.text: In carrying out the activities authorized under this subsection, the Secretary shall ensure proper security controls are in place to protect proprietary or sensitive information, as appropriate.

children.heading: Solar energy technology materials physical property database
children.label: 5 Solar energy technology materials physical property database

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than , the Secretary shall establish a comprehensive physical property database of materials for use in solar energy technologies, which shall identify the type, quantity, country of origin, source, significant uses, projected availability, and physical properties of materials used in solar energy technologies.September 1, 2022

children.label: i
children.text: other Department activities, including those carried out by the Office of Science;

children.label: ii
children.text: the Director of the National Institute of Standards and Technology;

children.label: iii
children.text: the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency;

children.label: iv
children.text: the Secretary of the Interior; and

children.label: v
children.text: relevant industry stakeholders, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Solar energy technology program strategic vision
children.label: 6 Solar energy technology program strategic vision

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than , and every 6 years thereafter, the Secretary shall submit to Congress a report on the strategic vision, progress, goals, and targets of the program, including assessments of solar energy markets and manufacturing.September 1, 2022

children.label: aa
children.text: new and advanced materials, such as cells made with new, high efficiency materials;

children.label: bb
children.text: solar module equipment and enabling technologies, including smart inverters, sensors, and tracking equipment; and

children.label: cc
children.text: innovative solar module designs and applications, including those that can directly integrate with new and existing buildings and other infrastructure; and

children.label: II
children.text: opportunities and barriers within the United States and international solar energy technology market;

children.label: ii
children.text: policy recommendations for enhancing solar energy technology manufacturing in the United States;

children.label: iii
children.text: a 10-year target and plan to enhance the competitiveness of solar energy technology manufacturing in the United States;

children.label: iv
children.text: a description of the technical and economic viability of siting solar energy technologies on current and former mine land, including necessary interconnection and transmission siting and the impact on local job creation; and

children.label: v
children.text: any other research areas as determined by the Secretary.

children.label: i
children.text: existing peer review processes;

children.label: ii
children.text: studies conducted by the National Laboratories; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the multiyear program planning required under .section 16358 of this title

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 7 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary to carry out the program $300,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2021 through 2025.
